title: MongoDB Sharding - Complete Guide (Beginner to Advanced)
description: Comprehensive guide on horizontal scaling through sharding, shard keys, and distributed data

sharding_overview.definition: Sharding distributes data across multiple MongoDB servers (shards) to handle large datasets and high throughput
sharding_overview.concept: Horizontal scaling - add more servers to scale, vs vertical scaling (bigger server)
purpose: Handle datasets larger than single server memory
Distribute write load across multiple servers
Improve query performance through parallel execution
key_components: Shard: Individual MongoDB instance holding subset of data
Shard key: Field used to distribute data across shards
Chunk: Range of shard key values on single shard
Mongos: Query router directing queries to correct shard
Config servers: Metadata about shard distribution (replica set)
Balancer: Automatically moves chunks between shards

definitions.definition: Individual MongoDB instance holding subset of data
Field used to determine which shard holds document
definitions.immutable: Cannot change shard key value after insert

definitions.definition: Range of shard key values on single shard
Query router that directs operations to correct shard
definitions.behavior: Looks up shard key, routes to appropriate shard

definitions.data: Which chunk on which shard, shard key info

section_2_beginner_sharding_setup.title: BEGINNER: Sharding Setup
section_2_beginner_sharding_setup.definition: Initialize sharding cluster with shards, config servers, and mongos
components_needed: 1+ mongos routers (can be multiple for load distribution)

beginner_examples.title: Connect to mongos and add shards

section_3_beginner_shard_key.title: BEGINNER: Choosing Shard Key
section_3_beginner_shard_key.definition: Shard key is field used to distribute data across shards
characteristics: Cardinality: Should have many unique values
Write distribution: Should distribute writes evenly

beginner_examples.title: Shard by studentId
beginner_examples.distribution: Each student's data on one shard

beginner_examples.title: Shard by major
beginner_examples.distribution: CS students on Shard1, Math on Shard2, etc.
beginner_examples.cons: Uneven distribution if majors not balanced

beginner_examples.title: Compound shard key
beginner_examples.distribution: Combination of major and GPA for distribution

section_4_beginner_querying.title: BEGINNER: Querying Sharded Collections
section_4_beginner_querying.definition: Queries routed through mongos to appropriate shard(s)

query_types.routing: Mongos uses shard key to route to single shard
Mongos gathers from all shards, merges

beginner_examples.title: Targeted query (fast)
beginner_examples.execution: Mongos routes to correct shard immediately

beginner_examples.title: Scatter-gather query (slower)
beginner_examples.execution: Mongos sends to all shards, merges results

section_5_intermediate_chunk_management.title: INTERMEDIATE: Chunk Management
section_5_intermediate_chunk_management.definition: Chunks are ranges of shard key values distributed across shards
chunk_splitting: MongoDB automatically splits chunks when they exceed 64MB

section_6_intermediate_balancing.title: INTERMEDIATE: Balancer & Data Distribution
section_6_intermediate_balancing.definition: Balancer automatically moves chunks to keep shards balanced
balancer_behavior: Runs on config server (replica set member)

intermediate_examples.title: Set balancer window

good_shard_keys.cons: Query by other fields requires scatter-gather

bad_shard_keys.problem: All writes go to last shard (hotspot)

section_8_advanced_hotspots.title: ADVANCED: Avoiding Hotspots
section_8_advanced_hotspots.definition: Hotspot: Single shard receiving disproportionate traffic

advanced_examples.title: Detect hotspot
advanced_examples.check: Compare document counts and chunk counts per shard

advanced_examples.title: Fix with compound key
advanced_examples.problem: Shard by major = CS gets 30% of writes
advanced_examples.solution: Shard by { major: 1, studentId: 1 } distributes within major

advanced_examples.title: Hash shard key
advanced_examples.effect: Hash distributes evenly even with sequential IDs

section_9_advanced_migration.title: ADVANCED: Adding Shards & Migration
section_9_advanced_migration.definition: Adding shards and rebalancing existing data

advanced_examples.title: Balancer automatically migrates data
advanced_examples.behavior: New shard is empty, balancer moves chunks to it

beginner.example: Student database grows from 100K to 1M documents

intermediate.solution: Shard by region, read from local shard
Shard by { date: 1, studentId: 1 } for range queries

intermediate.example: Each department needs own data subset
intermediate.solution: Shard by major, then query major subset

advanced.solution: Compound shard key with tenant ID

advanced.example: Ingest TB+ per day from 1000+ sources

practice_1_shard_key_planning.test: Test shard key on sample data before production

practice_2_avoid_low_cardinality.rule: Shard key should have many unique values

practice_3_even_distribution.rule: Data should distribute evenly across shards
practice_3_even_distribution.fix: Use hash sharding or compound key if skewed

practice_4_use_hash_for_monotonic.rule: Use hashed shard key for monotonically increasing values

practice_5_compound_keys_for_queries.example: { major: 1, gpa: 1 } for major range queries
practice_5_compound_keys_for_queries.benefit: First field used for routing, second for range

practice_6_monitor_balancing.rule: Monitor balancer to ensure even distribution

mistake_1_poor_cardinality.mistake: Shard by field with few unique values
mistake_1_poor_cardinality.problem: One shard gets 50% of data, other 50%
mistake_1_poor_cardinality.solution: Use high-cardinality field or compound key

mistake_2_monotonic_key.problem: All writes go to last shard (hotspot)

mistake_3_changing_shard_key.mistake: Try to change shard key after sharding
mistake_3_changing_shard_key.solution: Plan shard key carefully before sharding

mistake_4_ignoring_balancer.problem: Chunks not redistributed when adding shards

mistake_5_broadcasting_queries.mistake: Query without shard key, hits all shards
mistake_5_broadcasting_queries.solution: Design queries to include shard key when possible

mistake_6_no_monitoring.problem: Don't know if data is balanced

comparison.combined: Shard + Replicate: Each shard is replica set

summary.core_concept: Sharding distributes data across multiple servers for horizontal scaling
summary.minimum_setup: 2+ shards, config servers (replica set), mongos router
summary.key_decision: Choosing shard key (immutable, must have high cardinality)
summary.best_practice: Plan shard key carefully, use hash for monotonic values, monitor balancing
summary.progression: Beginner: Concepts and basic setup → Intermediate: Balancing and strategies → Advanced: Optimization and hotspots
